name: ResumeTweaker
description: An end-to-end AI-powered resume tailoring pipeline that parses uploaded PDFs and DOCX files, performs semantic gap analysis against a target job description, rephrases project bullets to include missing keywords, and exports the edited resume in the original format.
highlights: Engineered a vertical-first block-sorting algorithm using PyMuPDF (fitz) to extract block coordinates and preserve multi-column resume layout integrity — solving a fundamental failure mode of standard text parsers.
Replaced keyword frequency counting with contextual embedding similarity: integrated TensorFlow Hub with KeyBERT to compute cosine similarity scores between resume phrases and JD requirements.

Designed a decoupled LLM pipeline using the Claude API — local scripts handle extraction and gap mapping while the model is scoped strictly to rephrasing achievements, protecting PII and maximising edit precision.
Exposed the pipeline via FastAPI endpoints returning structured JSON; built a responsive Flask frontend for file upload, JD input, and real-time tailored resume output.

name: VibeTogether
description: A full-stack social platform that matches users based on shared hobbies and activities. Re-engineered from a hackathon prototype with improved architecture, data modeling, and feature scope.
highlights: Redesigned the application using Node.js, Express, React, and Next.js with MongoDB, introducing cleaner separation of concerns and more scalable schema design than the original build.
Extended session-based authentication, user registration, and profile matching logic to support activity-based discovery.
Improved input validation, session persistence, and API reliability across the full stack.

name: Emotion Detector
description: A full-stack web application using Flask and JavaScript to classify and visualise emotional sentiment from user-submitted text in real time using IBM Watson NLP.
highlights: Integrated IBM Watson NLP for multi-label emotion detection across multiple sentiment categories; designed FastAPI endpoints returning structured probability scores.
Implemented input validation and error handling to ensure reliable behaviour across real-world usage scenarios.

name: Road Network Simulation
description: Modelled the Carleton University campus road network as a weighted graph to simulate randomised traffic movement, congestion buildup, and evaluate congestion-reduction strategies.
highlights: Developed Vehicle and Controller classes to simulate randomised traffic movement, congestion buildup, and travel time distribution; visualised flow hotspots using NetworkX DiGraph and Matplotlib.
Evaluated three congestion-reduction strategies, achieving up to 11.19% improvement in average travel time through quantitative comparative analysis.

name: Neureset EEG Device
description: A desktop EEG simulation prototype in C++ with Qt, implementing real-time waveform visualisation via QCustomPlot and FFT-based frequency component analysis.
highlights: Applied OOP design principles across separated UI, header, and implementation files; used Qt signals and async wait functions to simulate live multi-threaded monitoring behaviour.
